exemplar.title: How does the specific heat capacity of water vary with its salinity?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the specific heat capacity of water vary with its salinity?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables and graphs are presented with clear titles, units, significant figures and RÂ² values, ensuring communication is both precise and clear.
Raw data recording includes uncertainties and headings; processed data shows consistent, accurate calculations without arithmetic errors.
Processing methods (efficiency calibration, specific heat calculation, regression) are appropriate and executed accurately.
weaknesses: Uncertainty propagation is shown in only one full example; averaged values lack explicit propagated errors.
Error bars are included on data points but not on the fitted regression line, limiting visualization of uncertainty in the model.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (communication and processing quality) fully satisfy the top band descriptors; although there are significant omissions in uncertainty propagation (strand 4), the overall data analysis is exceptionally clear, precise and accurate. Awarding the maximum mark of 6 reflects the high quality of presentation and processing.

strengths: Research question is clearly stated within a specific, appropriate context of salinity-driven thermal behaviour.
Methodology is detailed with quantitative values, controlled variables and uncertainties, allowing reproducibility.
Thoughtful consideration of methodological issues (heater efficiency, heat losses, stirring, dissolution) linked to data sufficiency.
weaknesses: Choice of 5% salinity increments and fixed volume lacks explicit justification in terms of resolution or statistical confidence.
Typographical inconsistencies in unit notation (e.g., âJ gâ»Â¹â° Câ»Â¹â) may confuse readers.
Procedure omits precise immersion depth for the thermometer, introducing minor ambiguity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The criterion overall sits comfortably in the 5â6 band. The research question is framed in a specific context (band 5), the methodology description is reproducible with minor omissions (band 5), and methodological considerations are well explained (band 4). Given the two strong top-band strands and one near-top band, a holistic mark of 5 balances the minor omissions against the clear strengths.

strengths: Conclusion is clearly justified by the analysis, restating the inverse relationship with numerical evidence consistent with the graphed data.
Comparison to literature values with percentage errors demonstrates relevant, justified engagement with scientific context.
weaknesses: Quantitative uncertainties (e.g., Â± on the slope) are not explicitly stated in the conclusion.
Comparison is limited to three data points; deeper theoretical discussion or additional sources would strengthen context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet high-level descriptors (5â6): the conclusion is justified and consistent with analysis, and the contextual comparison is relevant and justified. Minor omissions in uncertainty quantification and depth of context preclude the very top mark, resulting in a holistic 5.

strengths: Identifies and explains specific methodological weaknesses (heat loss, thermometer placement) and links them to their impact on results.
Proposes realistic and relevant improvements (better insulation, magnetic stirring) with qualitative explanations of expected gains.
Creative extensions suggest broader applicability to varied ionic systems.
weaknesses: Impact of improvements is not quantified (e.g., how much precision gain from insulation).
Suggested improvements do not reference specific materials or quantified performance gains.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both evaluation strands fall solidly in the 5â6 band by explaining the impact of limitations and describing realistic, relevant improvements. The absence of quantitative estimates of impact or specification of materials prevents a top-band 6, so a holistic mark of 5 accurately captures the high-quality evaluation with room for further precision.

strandSelections.reasoning: **The research question is clearly stated and immediately linked to salinityâdriven changes in the thermal behaviour of seawater**, a context that is both specific and appropriate for a Physics SL investigation.  The introduction explains why heat capacity matters, how ionic âcagingâ of water molecules provides a mechanistic expectation, and even hints at environmental relevance.  Because the discussion moves beyond a generic âwaterâ example to the concrete, real-world phenomenon of saline solutions, the strand comfortably meets the descriptor for the top band.  Minor omissions (e.g., no explicit mention of industrial or oceanographic applications) prevent awarding the very top of the range, so 5/6 is appropriate.

Uncertainties for mass, time and temperature are propagated in sample calculations; error bars are shown on the final graph; combined percentage uncertainties are quoted.  **Nevertheless, only one full propagation example is given and average values lack explicit propagated error**, so some significant omissions remain.  Band 3â4 is appropriate; evidence leans to the top of that band.

Methodological considerations such as heater efficiency, heat losses, uniform stirring, and dissolution assumptions are **described and linked to data sufficiency**, showing thought beyond a mere list.  However, the rationale for choosing the 0â25 % range and 5 % steps, or for fixing volume at 400 mL, is not deeply *explained*; likewise, no justification is provided for the chosen number of repeats in terms of statistical confidence.  Hence the strand fits the 3â4 band, awarded 4.

Heat loss and thermometer placement are **explained with clear links to their impact on underestimated specific heat capacity, particularly at higher salinity**.  The discussion goes beyond stating weaknesses to analysing how they skew results.  One or two additional quantified impacts would justify a 6; hence 5 fits best.

The student supplies **step-by-step procedures, full equipment list with uncertainties, controlled variables and trial structure**.  Volumes, masses, heating time and power are all quantified; three repeats at each of six salinities are specified; and a clear diagram is included.  A peer could reproduce the work with almost no questions.  Very small ambiguities (e.g., exact thermometer depth) justify 5 rather than 6.

Processing includes efficiency calibration of the heater, calculation of specific heat capacities for all trials, averaging, plotting, and linear regression.  **Calculations are internally consistent and free from obvious arithmetic errors; the method chosen is appropriate to the question.** Minor simplifications (assuming constant power after calibration) prevent a 6, but quality is well within the top band.

Improvements (better calorimeter insulation, magnetic stirring, repositioning thermometer) are **realistic, relevant and their expected effect on accuracy or precision is explained**.  The explanation is qualitative rather than quantitative, so a solid 5 in the top band is appropriate.

Raw data tables include units, measurement uncertainties and titles; processed tables are clearly headed; the graph is fully labelled with equation and RÂ². **Presentation is simultaneously clear (easy to read) and precise (correct significant figures, symbols, units).** Therefore the highest band is satisfied.

The student compares results with literature data at three salinity points, calculates percentage errors, and comments on trend agreement and growing deviations at high salinity. **This is a relevant, justified comparison to accepted scientific context.** Depth is good, though referencing more sources or a theoretical model would be needed for a 6.

The conclusion directly restates the observed inverse relationship and cites numerical evidence (e.g., decrease of ~0.37 J gâ»Â¹ Â°Câ»Â¹ per 0.05 g mLâ»Â¹).  It is fully consistent with the graphed analysis and addresses the hypothesis.  Because uncertainty ranges are acknowledged only qualitatively, a full 6 is not warranted, but the strand clearly meets the 5-6 descriptor.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and framed within a specific context of salinity-driven thermal behavior of seawater, demonstrating strong justification for the investigation.

The introduction provides a robust mechanistic explanation for ion âcagingâ and its effect on specific heat, linking theory to expected trends. Consider adding an explicit real-world application to deepen context.
Temperature change uncertainties appear as Â±1.0 Â°C but ignore propagation from initial and final readings. Include combined uncertainty calculation for ÎT.
The conclusion succinctly restates the inverse relationship and aligns with analysis. Including quantitative uncertainty (e.g. Â± on slope) would strengthen justification.
Suggested improvements for insulation and stirring are realistic and well explained. To further refine, propose specific materials or quantify expected precision gains.
Typographical inconsistencies in unit notation (e.g., âJ g^{-10}C^{-1}â) may confuse reproducibility. Ensure all units follow standard scientific format.
Controlled variables are well detailed with uncertainties, promoting reproducibility. To strengthen methodology, explain choice of 5 % salinity increments in terms of expected resolution.
The step-by-step methodology is comprehensive and reproducible. Consider specifying immersion heater insertion depth to eliminate ambiguity.
Figure 1 provides a clear visual of the setup but lacks dimensional labels. Adding scale indicators and a legend would improve clarity.
Assuming water density simplifies mass determination but introduces systematic error. Consider directly measuring solution mass to validate this assumption.
Raw data table includes appropriate uncertainties and clear headings, supporting precise data recording and processing.
The sample calculation for effective heater power is clear and correct, demonstrating precise use of formulas and units.
Only one full uncertainty propagation example is provided. To address omissions, include propagation for averaged effective power or for other trials.
Table 3 clearly presents specific heat capacities with uncertainties for each trial, facilitating transparent data processing.
Graph of specific heat vs. salinity includes equation and RÂ², indicating strong linear correlation. Consider adding error bars on the fitted line for uncertainty visualization.
Comparison to literature values with percentage errors provides relevant scientific context. To deepen, discuss potential theoretical models or additional sources.
Evaluation identifies heat loss impact well and links to underestimation of specific heat. Consider quantifying how much heat loss would alter results.
The extensions section offers creative and relevant directions for further investigation, expanding applicability to varied ionic systems.
Citations are relevant and correctly formatted. To enhance academic rigor, include primary research articles or recent reviews on heat capacity vs. salinity.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
